allies.rationale_text: Anchoring gives price-discrimination a way to shape perceived value before the final price is judged.
allies.source_quote: Setting an initial, high reference point

allies.rationale_text: Jobs-to-be-done helps price-discrimination separate segments by the outcome they hire the offer to achieve rather than by shallow demographics.
Pareto-principle helps price-discrimination focus differentiated effort on the highest-value customer segments.
allies.source_quote: focus differentiation efforts on the Power 4% of customers

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias can make price-discrimination defend a preferred pricing structure instead of testing what buyers actually value.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Price-discrimination conflicts with simplification when segmented pricing adds operational complexity that outweighs the marginal revenue captured.
structured_tensions.source_quote: price-discrimination conflicts with simplification (simplification) when segmented pricing adds operational complexity that outweighs the marginal revenue captured.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Price-discrimination conflicts with prospect-theory when reference-price anchoring causes consumers to perceive differentiated pricing as unfair loss rather than rational segmentation.
structured_tensions.source_quote: price-discrimination conflicts with prospect-theory (prospect theory) when reference-price anchoring causes consumers to perceive differentiated pricing as unfair loss rather than rational segmentation.
